title: Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore

overview.short: Hale Yedatore, a village in the Krishnaraja Nagara taluk of the Mysore district, Karnataka state, India is located about four kilometers north of the commercially important newly b
overview.description: Hale Yedatore, a village in the Krishnaraja Nagara taluk of the Mysore district, Karnataka state, India is located about four kilometers north of the commercially important newly built town of Krishnarajanagara, and about 44 km north-west of the historically important city of Mysore. The name Yedatore derives from two Kannada language words, yeda (lit, "left") and tore (lit, "river") – a name that arises from the "bend to the left" made by the river at that spot which is considered sacred by Hindus. There are steps leading from the temple down to the bathing areas ("ghats") by the river. Yedat

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore?
faq.a: The best time to visit Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (32 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore?
faq.a: Stays in Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Arakeshwara Temple, Haleyedatore?
faq.a: The top attractions are Krishnarajasagara, Krishnarajanagara, St. Anthony's Basilica, Dornahalli, Hebbal, Krishnarajanagara, Lakshmana Tirtha.
